tasks.subject: [dynamic-sidebar-content] [P1] Rename AgentSidebar to SessionSidebar and remove footer/rail rendering
tasks.description:
Rename `AgentSidebar` to `SessionSidebar` and strip out the footer/rail elements that will move to AppShell. ## Files to Change ### 1. Rename file: `apps/client/src/layers/features/session-list/ui/AgentSidebar.tsx` → `SessionSidebar.tsx` Use `git mv` to rename: ```bash git mv apps/client/src/layers/features/session-list/ui/AgentSidebar.tsx apps/client/src/layers/features/session-list/ui/SessionSidebar.tsx ``` ### 2. Update the component name and remove footer/rail from `SessionSidebar.tsx` The component is currently named `AgentSidebar`. Rename the export to `SessionSidebar`. Remove the following imports that are no longer needed (they move to AppShell): - `SidebarFooter` from `@/layers/shared/ui` - `SidebarRail` from `@/layers/shared/ui` - `SidebarFooterBar` from `./SidebarFooterBar` - `ProgressCard` and `useOnboarding` from `@/layers/features/onboarding` Remove the `shouldShowOnboarding` and `dismiss: dismissOnboarding` destructuring from the `useOnboarding()` call (the entire hook call is removed). Remove the `setOnboardingStep` from the `useAppStore()` destructuring. Remove the JSX at the end of the return that renders footer and rail: ```tsx // REMOVE this entire block from the return: {shouldShowOnboarding && (
setOnboardingStep(stepIndex)} onDismiss={dismissOnboarding} />
)} ``` The resulting component returns only: ```tsx export function SessionSidebar() { // ... all existing logic EXCEPT onboarding/footer/rail ... return ( <> Dashboard New session {formatShortcutKey(SHORTCUTS.NEW_SESSION)} {/* Sessions, Schedules, Connections tab panels — unchanged */} ); } ``` ### 3. Update barrel export: `apps/client/src/layers/features/session-list/index.ts` Change: ```typescript export { AgentSidebar } from './ui/AgentSidebar'; ``` To: ```typescript export { SessionSidebar } from './ui/SessionSidebar'; ``` Keep all other exports unchanged (`SessionItem`, `SessionsView`, `SidebarFooterBar`, `SidebarTabRow`). Note: `SidebarFooterBar` remains exported — AppShell will import it. Update the module-level TSDoc: ```typescript /** * Session list feature — sidebar for session management and directory selection. * * @module features/session-list */ export { SessionSidebar } from './ui/SessionSidebar'; export { SessionItem } from './ui/SessionItem'; export { SessionsView } from './ui/SessionsView'; export { SidebarFooterBar } from './ui/SidebarFooterBar'; export { SidebarTabRow } from './ui/SidebarTabRow'; ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - `AgentSidebar.tsx` no longer exists; `SessionSidebar.tsx` exists with same logic minus footer/rail - `SessionSidebar` does not import from `@/layers/features/onboarding` - `SessionSidebar` does not render `SidebarFooter`, `SidebarRail`, `ProgressCard`, or `SidebarFooterBar` - `features/session-list/index.ts` exports `SessionSidebar` (not `AgentSidebar`) - `pnpm typecheck` passes - No references to `AgentSidebar` remain in `session-list/` (except test file, updated in Phase 4)
tasks.activeForm: Renaming AgentSidebar to SessionSidebar and stripping footer/rail

tasks.subject: [dynamic-sidebar-content] [P1] Move footer rendering from SessionSidebar to AppShell
tasks.description:
Move `SidebarFooter`, `ProgressCard`, `SidebarFooterBar`, and `SidebarRail` rendering from the former `AgentSidebar` into `AppShell.tsx`. These elements become static chrome that persists across all routes (never inside AnimatePresence). ## File to Change: `apps/client/src/AppShell.tsx` ### New Imports Add these imports: ```typescript import { SidebarFooterBar } from '@/layers/features/session-list'; import { ProgressCard } from '@/layers/features/onboarding'; import { // ... existing imports ... SidebarFooter, SidebarRail, } from '@/layers/shared/ui'; ``` Note: `useOnboarding` is already imported in AppShell. The `shouldShowOnboarding` value is already computed. The `setOnboardingStep` action needs to be available — check if AppShell already destructures it from `useAppStore()`. If not, add: ```typescript const setOnboardingStep = useAppStore((s) => s.setOnboardingStep); ``` And `dismissOnboarding` is needed from `useOnboarding()`. The current AppShell already has: ```typescript const { shouldShowOnboarding, isLoading: isOnboardingLoading } = useOnboarding(); ``` Update this to also destructure `dismiss`: ```typescript const { shouldShowOnboarding, isLoading: isOnboardingLoading, dismiss: dismissOnboarding } = useOnboarding(); ``` ### JSX Changes Replace the current sidebar rendering: ```tsx ``` With (for now, Phase 1 only — the AnimatePresence wrapper comes in Phase 2): ```tsx {/* ── Static footer — never animates ── */} {shouldShowOnboarding && (
setOnboardingStep(stepIndex)} onDismiss={dismissOnboarding} />
)} ``` Also update the import to use `SessionSidebar` instead of `AgentSidebar`: ```typescript // Change: import { AgentSidebar } from '@/layers/features/session-list'; // To: import { SessionSidebar, SidebarFooterBar } from '@/layers/features/session-list'; ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - AppShell renders `` (not ``) - AppShell renders `SidebarFooter` with `ProgressCard` and `SidebarFooterBar` inside the ``, after `` - AppShell renders `` after the footer - The `onboardingDismissed` / `handleOnboardingComplete` logic remains unchanged (it gates the onboarding flow, not the progress card) - Visual behavior is identical to before — sidebar looks the same, footer is in the same position - `pnpm typecheck` passes - No import of `AgentSidebar` remains in `AppShell.tsx`
tasks.activeForm: Moving footer and rail rendering to AppShell

tasks.subject: [dynamic-sidebar-content] [P2] Create SessionHeader and DashboardHeader components
[dynamic-sidebar-content] [P2] Create DashboardSidebar feature module with placeholder content
tasks.description:
Create the `features/dashboard-sidebar/` FSD feature module with a minimal placeholder sidebar for the dashboard route. This is a new feature module — self-contained sidebar content, not a composition of other features. ## New Directory Structure ``` apps/client/src/layers/features/dashboard-sidebar/ ├── ui/ │ └── DashboardSidebar.tsx └── index.ts ``` ## New File: `apps/client/src/layers/features/dashboard-sidebar/ui/DashboardSidebar.tsx` ```tsx import { SidebarHeader, SidebarContent, SidebarMenu, SidebarMenuItem, SidebarMenuButton, } from '@/layers/shared/ui'; import { LayoutDashboard, MessageSquare } from 'lucide-react'; import { useNavigate } from '@tanstack/react-router'; /** * Dashboard sidebar — navigation and overview for the dashboard route. * Placeholder content; full design is a follow-up spec. */ export function DashboardSidebar() { const navigate = useNavigate(); return ( <> Dashboard navigate({ to: '/session' })} className="text-muted-foreground hover:bg-accent hover:text-foreground flex w-full items-center gap-1.5 rounded-md px-2.5 py-1.5 text-xs font-medium transition-all duration-100 active:scale-[0.98]" > Sessions

Agent overview coming soon

 ); } ``` ## New File: `apps/client/src/layers/features/dashboard-sidebar/index.ts` ```typescript /** * Dashboard sidebar feature — navigation and overview for the dashboard route. * * @module features/dashboard-sidebar */ export { DashboardSidebar } from './ui/DashboardSidebar'; ``` ## FSD Compliance Notes - This is a `features/` layer module — it can import from `entities/` and `shared/` - It imports only from `@/layers/shared/ui` (sidebar primitives) and `@tanstack/react-router` (external package) - It does NOT import from other features — clean isolation - `lucide-react` icons are external packages, not layer violations ## Acceptance Criteria - `DashboardSidebar` component renders a sidebar header with "Dashboard" (active) and "Sessions" (navigates to `/session`) nav items - Clicking "Sessions" calls `navigate({ to: '/session' })` - The sidebar content area shows placeholder text "Agent overview coming soon" - The barrel `index.ts` exports `DashboardSidebar` - `pnpm typecheck` passes - No FSD layer violations (no imports from other features or widgets)

tasks.subject: [dynamic-sidebar-content] [P2] Update embedded mode App.tsx to use SessionSidebar
tasks.description:
Update the embedded mode `App.tsx` (Obsidian plugin shell) to import `SessionSidebar` instead of `AgentSidebar`. This is a simple import rename — no behavioral changes.

## File to Change: `apps/client/src/App.tsx`

### Change the import:
```typescript
// Change:
import { AgentSidebar } from '@/layers/features/session-list';
// To:
import { SessionSidebar } from '@/layers/features/session-list';
```

### Change the JSX (two occurrences):

The component `<AgentSidebar />` is rendered inside the overlay sidebar. Replace both occurrences:
```tsx
// Change:
<AgentSidebar />
// To:
<SessionSidebar />
```

There is one occurrence at approximately line 116 inside the overlay sidebar motion.div.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `App.tsx` imports `SessionSidebar` from `@/layers/features/session-list`
- `App.tsx` renders `<SessionSidebar />` (not `<AgentSidebar />`)
- No behavioral changes to embedded mode
- `pnpm typecheck` passes
- No references to `AgentSidebar` remain in `App.tsx`
tasks.activeForm: Updating embedded mode to use SessionSidebar

[dynamic-sidebar-content] [P3] Final cleanup — remove stale AgentSidebar references and update documentation
tasks.description:
Sweep the codebase for any remaining references to `AgentSidebar` and update internal documentation to reflect the new sidebar/header slot pattern.

## Codebase Sweep

Search for `AgentSidebar` across the entire codebase:
```bash
grep -r 'AgentSidebar' apps/client/src/ --include='*.ts' --include='*.tsx'
```

Expected locations that need updating (if not already handled by prior tasks):

1. **`apps/client/src/App.tsx`** — Should already use `SessionSidebar` (task 2.4)
2. **`apps/client/src/AppShell.tsx`** — Should already use `SessionSidebar` (task 1.2)
3. **`apps/client/src/layers/features/session-list/index.ts`** — Should already export `SessionSidebar` (task 1.1)
4. **Test files** — Should already be renamed (task 3.1)

If any straggler references remain (e.g., in comments, type names, or other files), update them.

## Remove deprecated re-export (if present)

If task 1.1 added a deprecated `AgentSidebar` re-export for safety, remove it now:
```typescript
// REMOVE if present in session-list/index.ts:
/** @deprecated Use SessionSidebar instead */
export { SessionSidebar as AgentSidebar } from './ui/SessionSidebar';
```

## Documentation Updates

### 1. `contributing/project-structure.md`

Add the new `dashboard-sidebar` feature module to the FSD features list. Find the features section and add:
```
dashboard-sidebar/     # Dashboard route sidebar (navigation, overview placeholder)
```

### 2. `contributing/architecture.md`

Add a brief note about the sidebar/header slot pattern under the client architecture section. Example addition:
```
### Route-Aware Sidebar & Header Slots

AppShell uses private switch hooks (`useSidebarSlot`, `useHeaderSlot`) to render
route-specific sidebar and header content. The hooks read `useRouterState` and
return the appropriate component keyed for AnimatePresence cross-fade transitions.
The sidebar footer (branding, settings) and sidebar rail are static chrome rendered
outside the AnimatePresence wrapper.
```

### 3. `AGENTS.md`

In the Client section under Routing, the route descriptions should mention the sidebar:
```
- `/` → `DashboardPage` + `DashboardSidebar` + `DashboardHeader`
- `/session` → `SessionPage` + `SessionSidebar` + `SessionHeader`
```

## Final Verification

Run the full test suite and typecheck:
```bash
pnpm typecheck
pnpm test -- --run
```

All tests should pass. No `AgentSidebar` references should remain anywhere except potentially in git history.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Zero references to `AgentSidebar` in `apps/client/src/` (search with grep confirms)
- `contributing/project-structure.md` lists `dashboard-sidebar/` feature module
- `contributing/architecture.md` describes the slot pattern
- `AGENTS.md` route descriptions updated
- `pnpm typecheck` passes
- `pnpm test -- --run` passes with no failures
tasks.activeForm: Cleaning up stale references and updating documentation
